title: [InstCombine] foldVecExtTruncToExtElt - extend to handle trunc(lshr(extractelement(x,c1),c2)) -> extractelement(bitcast(x),c3) patterns.

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions shift support extension but no known tests demonstrate the lshr pattern. This tests the trunc(lshr(extractelement)) pattern with shift amount that is a multiple of destination bits.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i8 @trunc_lshr_extract_i32_to_i8_shift16(<4 x i32> %x) {
  %1 = bitcast <4 x i32> %x to <16 x i8>
  %t = extractelement <16 x i8> %1, i64 2
  ret i8 %t
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i8 @trunc_lshr_extract_i32_to_i8_shift16(<4 x i32> %x) {
#0:
  %e = extractelement <4 x i32> %x, i32 0
  %s = lshr i32 %e, 16
  %t = trunc i32 %s to i8
  ret i8 %t
}
=>
define i8 @trunc_lshr_extract_i32_to_i8_shift16(<4 x i32> %x) {
#0:
  %#1 = bitcast <4 x i32> %x to <16 x i8>
  %t = extractelement <16 x i8> %#1, i64 2
  ret i8 %t
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: This test exercises the NEW lshr + trunc pattern added in PR #109689. None of the known tests cover the lshr case - they only test direct trunc(extractelement). This is the primary new functionality from the patch.

tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests i64→i8 truncation ratio (8:1) with lshr, which differs from known tests that only cover i64→i32 (2:1) or i64→i16 (4:1). The combination of larger truncation ratio with shift amount is not covered.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i8 @lshr_trunc_i64_to_i8_shift32(<2 x i64> %x) {
  %1 = bitcast <2 x i64> %x to <16 x i8>
  %t = extractelement <16 x i8> %1, i64 12
  ret i8 %t
}

  %t = trunc i64 %s to i8
